name: Barbecue Sauce - Norine Juenger - Lenzburg
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the tomato sauce, brown sugar, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, salt, paprika, dry mustard, chili powder, and cayenne pepper in a large saucepan.
2. Mix well to combine all ingredients.
3. Place the saucepan over medium heat and bring the mixture to a simmer.
4. Allow the sauce to simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly and flavors meld together.
5. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
6. Serve hot. Makes approximately 2 1/2 cups.
While there was no indication in the original recipe, this barbecue sauce is best suited for use with pork dishes, as that is traditionally associated with BBQ in that region.
